column1: ________Ireland is one of the two large islands of________British Isles.
It is surrounded in the north, west and south by____________Atlantic Ocean.
In the east it is separated from_________Great Britain by_______Irish Sea, which narrows to_________North Channel in the North and St. George's Channel in the South.
Irish mountains are low; the highest,___________Carrantuohill, is only 3414 feet high.
There are many small islands in it,_________Lough Corrib and__________Lough Mask are two of the largest.
Most of the islands is occupied by___________Irish Republic.
The capital of the country is_______Dublin situated on the banks of________Liffey.
__________Dublin is an attractive city, with wide streets and squares.
The city spreads out in all directions, even to the northernmost foothills of_________Wicklow Mountains.
___________Dublin is the home of the oldest university in_________Ireland, ___________Trinity College, founded in 1591.

_________National University of________Ireland is also located in_________Dublin, as are important libraries, galleries, museums and theatres like_________Irish Literary Theatre or____________Abbey Theatre.
